title: Historical AI Workflow Boundary And Relay-Reduction First-Slice Planning
historical_note: This record preserves one earlier Dev Residency transition slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform on 2026-05-07. It is historical provenance, not a live mutation-seam or AI-boundary transition surface for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this record only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier Dev Residency 036 transition slice preserved here
objective: Select the first bounded Olares-first AI workflow slice that can reduce stakeholder relay burden using already-present repo surfaces, while preserving the current no-go boundaries around Codex admission, broad AI-services expansion, and hosting transition.
dependency_note: Packet 035 made Olares-first workflow leverage the near-term priority. The smallest truthful next move is not a large AI-services rollout, because the repo already contains a canary-proven minimal MCP trio and an `apex-jobs` run ledger with the env and promotion controls Step 2 needed to see in writing.
decision_requirements: Prefer already-present repo surfaces over new service installs.
Keep the first slice bounded to Claude-compatible workflow hardening plus the minimum MCP and ledger boundary needed to reduce relay burden.
Preserve `apex-jobs` as the explicit env and promotion trust boundary for the current slice.
Do not admit Codex, Ollama, Dify, n8n, or Gitea into the first slice by implication.
Name exactly one execution packet after the planning decision.
decision_rationale: The repo already contains the admitted minimal MCP trio shape in executable form: `apex-fs`, `apex-db`, and `apex-jobs` are wired in `infra/compose.dev.yml`, `tools/run-canary.ps1`, `tools/run-canary.sh`, and the canary contract snapshot.

`apex-jobs` already implements the `env=sandbox|host` run ledger plus `promote_packet` refusal logic, making it the best current trust boundary for the first slice even though the older Supabase orchestration protocol still assumes a Supabase-first task queue.

Claude Code remains the only first-slice-ready AI execution surface under the Step 2 assessment, so the first slice should optimize Claude-compatible operator flow instead of reopening the unresolved Codex decision.

A bounded operator kit that starts, verifies, and records the minimal trio can reduce human relay burden immediately because it gives the AI workflow a concrete, repeatable control surface rather than relying on ad hoc packet prose or user-mediated restatement.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Dev Residency 037 - Minimal MCP Trio Operator Surface And AI Boundary Restatement Execution
closure_note: Executed 2026-05-06 as the first planning surface after the Packet 035 priority reset.
